title: Make a Simple Budget
summary: Make a simple budget so you know where your money is going.
learnerPromise: Make a simple budget so you know where your money is going.
whyItMatters: A budget is not a restriction. It is a plan that makes sure your money goes where you actually want it to go. It turns anxiety into confidence.
realLifeUses: Make sure rent and bills are covered before spending on fun.
Set aside money for savings every month.
Prepare for an irregular income from freelance or part-time work.
Save for a big purchase without going into debt.
youWillLearn: Add up your total monthly income from all sources.
List all monthly expenses and separate needs from wants.
Use the 50/30/20 rule as a starting framework.
Track actual spending against your budget and adjust.
miniChallenge: Create a simple budget for $20, $50, or your next paycheck.
steps: Write down all money you expect to receive — every source.
List every expense you know is coming: rent, bills, groceries, transport.
Apply the 50/30/20 framework: 50% needs, 30% wants, 20% savings.
Adjust the percentages to fit your real life. Make sure needs are covered first.
Write the final budget down. Commit to checking it weekly.
completionCriteria: You have a written budget with specific amounts for needs, wants, and savings.
Your budget totals match your expected income — every dollar is assigned.
commonProblems: Your expenses are higher than your income — cut wants first, then find ways to reduce needs.
Irregular income makes budgeting hard — budget based on your lowest expected month.
You forget irregular expenses like annual subscriptions — divide yearly costs by 12.
tips: Pay yourself first — put savings aside the day you get paid, not at the end of the month.
Use cash envelopes for wants if you tend to overspend with cards.
Review and adjust your budget every month. It is a living document, not a rule carved in stone.
